name: BMI Calculator
tagline: Calculate your Body Mass Index and health category
keywords: bmi calculator, body mass index
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var h=(f('heightFt')*12+f('heightIn'))*0.0254;var bmi=w/(h*h);var cat=bmi<18.5?'Underweight':bmi<25?'Normal':bmi<30?'Overweight':'Obese';var lo=Math.round(18.5*h*h/0.453592);var hi=Math.round(24.9*h*h/0.453592);return{bmiVal:fmt(bmi,1),category:cat,healthy:lo+' - '+hi+' lbs'};

faqs.a: Body Mass Index is weight in kg divided by height in meters squared. It screens for weight categories but does not directly measure body fat.
Underweight: below 18.5. Normal: 18.5-24.9. Overweight: 25-29.9. Obese: 30+.
No — athletes with high muscle mass may show overweight BMI despite low body fat. Use body fat percentage for a more complete picture.
Research shows lowest mortality risk at BMI 22-25. However, overall health depends on many factors beyond weight.

howItWorksIntro: This bmi calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Ideal Weight Calculator
tagline: Find your ideal weight range based on height and frame size
keywords: ideal weight calculator, healthy weight
calcJS: var h=(f('heightFt')*12+f('heightIn'));var male=f('sex')===1;var hamwi=male?106+(h-60)*6:100+(h-60)*5;var devine=male?110.23+(h-60)*5.07:100.31+(h-60)*5.07;var robinson=male?115.13+(h-60)*4.18:108.03+(h-60)*3.64;var hm=h*0.0254;var lo=Math.round(18.5*hm*hm*2.205);var hi=Math.round(24.9*hm*hm*2.205);return{hamwi:fmt(hamwi,0)+' lbs',devine:fmt(devine,0)+' lbs',robinson:fmt(robinson,0)+' lbs',range:lo+' - '+hi+' lbs'};

faqs.q: Which ideal weight formula is best?
faqs.a: No single formula is perfect. We show multiple formulas so you can see the range. The BMI-based range (18.5-24.9) is the most widely used clinical standard.
Yes. Large-framed individuals naturally weigh more. Measure your wrist: under 6.5 inches is small frame, 6.5-7.5 is medium, over 7.5 is large for men.

faqs.q: Is ideal weight the same as healthiest weight?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. Health depends on body composition, fitness level, and metabolic markers — not just scale weight. A fit person at a higher weight can be healthier than a sedentary person at ideal weight.
The formulas vary by 10-15 lbs for the same height. Your personal ideal depends on muscle mass, body frame, age, and how you feel.

howItWorksIntro: This ideal weight calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Calorie Calculator
tagline: Calculate how many calories you need per day based on your goals
keywords: calorie calculator, daily calories, calorie needs
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var h=(f('heightFt')*12+f('heightIn'))*2.54;var age=f('age');var male=f('sex')===1;var bmr=male?10*w+6.25*h-5*age+5:10*w+6.25*h-5*age-161;var factors=[1.2,1.375,1.55,1.725,1.9];var mult=factors[Math.min(Math.max(f('activity')-1,0),4)];var maintain=bmr*mult;return{bmr:fmt(bmr,0)+' cal',maintain:fmt(maintain,0)+' cal',lose:fmt(maintain-500,0)+' cal',gain:fmt(maintain+500,0)+' cal'};

faqs.a: We use the Mifflin-St Jeor equation to find your BMR (basal metabolic rate), then multiply by an activity factor. This is the most accurate widely-used formula.
Basal Metabolic Rate is the calories your body burns at complete rest — just to keep organs functioning. It accounts for 60-70% of total daily calories.
Mifflin-St Jeor is accurate to within 10% for most people. Individual variation depends on muscle mass, genetics, and metabolic health.

faqs.q: Should I eat 500 fewer calories to lose weight?
faqs.a: A 500 calorie daily deficit produces roughly 1 lb/week loss. Do not go below 1,200 (women) or 1,500 (men) calories without medical supervision.

howItWorksIntro: This calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Calories Burned Calculator
tagline: Estimate calories burned during various activities
keywords: calories burned calculator, exercise calories
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var met=f('activity');var min=f('minutes');var cal=met*w*3.5/200*min;return{burned:fmt(cal,0)+' cal',perHour:fmt(cal/min*60,0)+' cal/hr',fatLbs:fmt(cal/3500,3)+' lbs'};

faqs.a: Metabolic Equivalent of Task. MET 1 = sitting still. Walking = 3-4 MET, cycling = 6-8, running = 8-12, swimming = 6-10. Higher MET = more intense.
MET-based estimates are within 15-20% for most people. Actual burn depends on intensity, fitness level, body composition, and environmental factors.

faqs.q: How many calories to lose 1 pound?
faqs.a: Roughly 3,500 calories equals 1 pound of fat. A 500 cal/day deficit through diet and exercise produces about 1 lb/week loss.
Yes — EPOC (excess post-exercise oxygen consumption) can burn 6-15% additional calories after intense exercise. This calculator estimates direct burn only.

howItWorksIntro: This calories burned calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Walking Calorie Calculator
tagline: Calculate calories burned walking based on speed, weight, and time
keywords: walking calorie calculator, calories walking
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var spd=f('speed');var min=f('minutes');var met=spd<3?2.5:spd<3.5?3.3:spd<4?3.8:spd<4.5?5:6.3;var cal=met*w*3.5/200*min;var dist=spd*min/60;var steps=Math.round(dist*2000);return{burned:fmt(cal,0)+' cal',distance:fmt(dist,2)+' miles',steps:fmt(steps,0)};

faqs.q: How many calories does walking burn?
faqs.a: A 170 lb person walking at 3.5 mph burns about 300 calories per hour. Faster pace and higher body weight increase the burn.

faqs.q: How many steps in a mile?
faqs.a: Approximately 2,000 steps per mile at average stride length. Taller people take fewer steps, shorter people take more.
Walking is excellent low-impact exercise. 30 minutes daily reduces heart disease risk by 19%, improves mood, and burns meaningful calories. 10,000 steps/day is a good target.

faqs.q: Walking vs running for weight loss?
faqs.a: Running burns more calories per minute, but walking is sustainable for longer. Net calorie burn per mile is similar. Walking has lower injury risk.

howItWorksIntro: This walking calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Steps to Calories Calculator
tagline: Convert your daily step count to calories burned
keywords: steps to calories, how many calories per step
calcJS: var steps=f('steps');var w=f('weight');var miles=steps/2000;var cal=miles*w*0.57;var min=Math.round(miles/3.5*60);return{calories:fmt(cal,0)+' cal',miles:fmt(miles,1),minutes:fmt(min,0)+' min'};

faqs.q: How many calories does 10,000 steps burn?
faqs.a: For a 170 lb person, roughly 485 calories. This varies by weight, walking speed, and terrain.

faqs.q: How many steps should I walk per day?
faqs.a: 10,000 steps is a common goal but studies show significant health benefits starting at 7,500 steps. More is better up to about 12,000 steps.
About 5 miles for average stride length. This takes roughly 80-100 minutes of walking.
Yes — any movement counts. Cleaning, cooking, and shopping all contribute to your daily step total and calorie burn.

howItWorksIntro: This steps to calories calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Bench Press Max Calculator
tagline: Estimate your bench press one-rep max from any rep range
keywords: bench press calculator, 1 rep max bench
calcJS: var w=f('weight');var r=f('reps');var orm=w*(1+r/30);var f5=orm*0.87;var f10=orm*0.75;return{oneRM:fmt(orm,0)+' lbs',fiveRM:fmt(f5,0)+' lbs',tenRM:fmt(f10,0)+' lbs'};

faqs.a: We use the Epley formula: 1RM = Weight × (1 + Reps/30). It is most accurate for 2-10 reps. For 1 rep, the weight IS your max.
Testing a true 1RM carries injury risk. Using a calculator with a 3-5 rep max gives a very accurate estimate without the danger of maximal singles.

faqs.q: What is a good bench press?
faqs.a: Intermediate benchmarks: body weight is solid, 1.5× body weight is advanced, 2× body weight is elite. Most gym-goers fall between 0.75-1.25× body weight.
Every 8-12 weeks. More frequent testing interrupts training progression without providing useful data.

howItWorksIntro: This bench press max calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Squat Max Calculator
tagline: Estimate your squat one-rep max
keywords: squat calculator, squat 1 rep max
calcJS: var w=f('weight');var r=f('reps');var orm=w*(1+r/30);return{oneRM:fmt(orm,0)+' lbs',fiveRM:fmt(orm*0.87,0)+' lbs',tenRM:fmt(orm*0.75,0)+' lbs'};

faqs.a: For men: 1.5× body weight is intermediate, 2× is advanced, 2.5× is elite. For women: 1× BW is intermediate, 1.5× is advanced.
Epley formula is accurate within 5% for 2-10 reps. High rep sets (15+) tend to overestimate.
Front squat is typically 80-85% of back squat max. Different movement patterns stress quads and core differently.

faqs.q: How do I increase my squat?
faqs.a: Progressive overload: add 5 lbs per session for beginners, 5 lbs per week for intermediates. Focus on depth, form, and consistent training.

howItWorksIntro: This squat max calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.

name: Deadlift Max Calculator
tagline: Estimate your deadlift one-rep max
keywords: deadlift calculator, deadlift 1rm
calcJS: var w=f('weight');var r=f('reps');var orm=w*(1+r/30);return{oneRM:fmt(orm,0)+' lbs',fiveRM:fmt(orm*0.87,0)+' lbs',tenRM:fmt(orm*0.75,0)+' lbs'};

faqs.a: For men: 2× body weight is intermediate, 2.5× is advanced, 3× is elite. The deadlift is typically the heaviest lift for most people.
Both are valid competition lifts. Sumo uses a wider stance and shorter range of motion. Neither is inherently better — try both and use what feels strongest.
Maintain a neutral spine, brace your core, keep the bar close to your body, and do not round your lower back. Start light and perfect your form.
1-2 times per week is optimal for most people. The deadlift is very taxing on the nervous system and requires adequate recovery.

name: Marathon Pace Calculator
tagline: Calculate your marathon pace and predicted finish time
keywords: marathon pace calculator, marathon time
calcJS: var totalMin=f('hours')*60+f('minutes');var pace=totalMin/26.2;var paceMin=Math.floor(pace);var paceSec=Math.round((pace-paceMin)*60);var fiveK=pace*3.107;var half=totalMin/2;return{pace:paceMin+':'+String(paceSec).padStart(2,'0')+' /mile',fiveK:Math.floor(fiveK)+':'+String(Math.round((fiveK-Math.floor(fiveK))*60)).padStart(2,'0'),halfSplit:Math.floor(half)+':'+String(Math.round((half-Math.floor(half))*60)).padStart(2,'0'),perMile:fmt(pace*60,0)+' sec'};

faqs.q: What is a good marathon time?
faqs.a: Average finish is about 4:30. Sub-4:00 is solid, sub-3:30 is competitive, sub-3:00 is elite amateur, and sub-2:30 is professional territory.
Yes — running the same pace throughout is the most efficient strategy. Negative splits (faster second half) are even better but harder to execute.

faqs.q: How do I train for my goal pace?
faqs.a: Long runs at 30-60 seconds slower than goal pace. Tempo runs at goal pace. Speed work at faster than goal pace. Build up to 18-20 mile long runs.

faqs.q: What about the wall at mile 20?
faqs.a: The wall happens when glycogen stores deplete. Prevent it with proper nutrition (carb loading, race-day fueling with gels every 45 min) and adequate training.

howItWorksIntro: This marathon pace calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Half Marathon Pace Calculator
tagline: Calculate your half marathon pace and splits
keywords: half marathon pace calculator, half marathon time
calcJS: var totalMin=f('hours')*60+f('minutes');var pace=totalMin/13.1;var paceMin=Math.floor(pace);var paceSec=Math.round((pace-paceMin)*60);var fiveK=pace*3.107;var tenK=pace*6.214;return{pace:paceMin+':'+String(paceSec).padStart(2,'0')+' /mile',fiveK:Math.floor(fiveK)+':'+String(Math.round((fiveK-Math.floor(fiveK))*60)).padStart(2,'0'),tenK:Math.floor(tenK)+':'+String(Math.round((tenK-Math.floor(tenK))*60)).padStart(2,'0')};

faqs.q: What is a good half marathon time?
faqs.a: Average is about 2:00-2:15. Sub-1:45 is strong, sub-1:30 is competitive, sub-1:15 is elite.
Double your half time and add 10-20 minutes for a marathon estimate. The half is a good predictor of marathon potential.
12-16 weeks for a beginner who can already run 3-4 miles. Includes building up to a 10-12 mile long run.

faqs.q: What pace should long runs be?
faqs.a: 30-90 seconds per mile slower than goal race pace. Long runs build endurance — speed comes from tempo and interval sessions.

howItWorksIntro: This half marathon pace calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: 5K Pace Calculator
tagline: Calculate your 5K pace and predicted finish time
keywords: 5k pace calculator, 5k time
calcJS: var totalSec=f('minutes')*60+f('seconds');var pacePerMile=totalSec/3.107;var pm=Math.floor(pacePerMile/60);var ps=Math.round(pacePerMile%60);var pacePerKm=totalSec/5;var km=Math.floor(pacePerKm/60);var ks=Math.round(pacePerKm%60);var mph=3.107/(totalSec/3600);return{pace:pm+':'+String(ps).padStart(2,'0')+' /mile',paceKm:km+':'+String(ks).padStart(2,'0')+' /km',speed:fmt(mph,1)+' mph'};

faqs.a: Beginner: 30-35 min. Intermediate: 22-28 min. Advanced: 18-22 min. Elite: under 16 min. Average US race finisher: about 28 minutes.

faqs.q: How do I run a faster 5K?
faqs.a: Interval training is key. Run 400m repeats at target pace with rest. Also do tempo runs at slightly slower than race pace for 20-30 minutes.
Double your 5K time and add 1-3 minutes. A 25-minute 5K typically translates to a 52-53 minute 10K.

faqs.q: How often can I race a 5K?
faqs.a: Every 2-4 weeks. The 5K is short enough that recovery is quick. Racing frequently is good practice for pacing and mental toughness.

howItWorksIntro: This 5k pace calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Cycling Power Calculator
tagline: Calculate cycling power-to-weight ratio and FTP zones
keywords: cycling power calculator, watts per kg, FTP
calcJS: var ftp=f('ftp');var kg=f('weight')*0.453592;var wpkg=ftp/kg;return{wpkg:fmt(wpkg,2)+' W/kg',zone2:fmt(ftp*0.56,0)+'-'+fmt(ftp*0.75,0)+' W',zone4:fmt(ftp*0.91,0)+'-'+fmt(ftp*1.05,0)+' W',zone5:fmt(ftp*1.06,0)+'-'+fmt(ftp*1.20,0)+' W'};

faqs.a: Functional Threshold Power — the maximum average power you can sustain for one hour. It is the gold standard for cycling fitness and training zone calculation.
Recreational: 2-3 W/kg. Cat 3 racer: 3.5-4. Cat 1: 4.5-5. Tour de France riders: 6+ W/kg. Weight matters as much as raw watts.
20-minute all-out effort on a trainer or flat road. FTP = average power × 0.95. Or use a ramp test in Zwift/TrainerRoad.
Sweet spot training (88-94% of FTP) for 20-60 minutes. Threshold intervals at 95-105% for 8-20 minutes. Consistent training over months.

howItWorksIntro: This cycling power calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Training zones based on percentage of FTP
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Push-Up Test Calculator
tagline: Rate your fitness level based on push-up count
keywords: push up test, pushup fitness test
calcJS: var pu=f('pushups');var age=f('age');var male=f('sex')===1;var good,excel;if(male){good=age<30?27:age<40?21:age<50?16:age<60?12:10;excel=age<30?36:age<40?30:age<50?25:age<60?20:18;}else{good=age<30?14:age<40?13:age<50?11:age<60?6:6;excel=age<30?30:age<40?27:age<50?24:age<60?17:12;}var rating=pu>=excel?'Excellent':pu>=good?'Good':pu>=good*0.6?'Average':'Below Average';var pctile=pu>=excel?'Top 20%':pu>=good?'Top 40%':pu>=good*0.6?'Middle 40%':'Bottom 20%';var next=pu<good?good:pu<excel?excel:excel+10;return{rating:rating,percentile:pctile,goal:fmt(next,0)+' push-ups'};

faqs.q: How many push-ups should I be able to do?
faqs.a: Varies by age and sex. For a 30-year-old male: 20+ is average, 27+ is good, 36+ is excellent. Standards decrease with age.
Hands slightly wider than shoulders, body in a straight line from head to heels, chest touches the floor, full arm extension at top. No sagging hips or raised butt.

faqs.q: How do I increase my push-up count?
faqs.a: Do push-ups 3-4 times per week. Try pyramid sets (1,2,3...10...3,2,1), grease-the-groove (multiple small sets throughout the day), or progressive overload with weighted push-ups.

faqs.q: Are push-ups a good fitness test?
faqs.a: Yes — they test upper body strength, core stability, and muscular endurance. Military and fire departments use push-up tests as fitness assessments.

howItWorksIntro: This push-up test calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Rating based on ACSM (American College of Sports Medicine) norms by age and sex
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Body Recomposition Calculator
tagline: Calculate calories and macros for simultaneous fat loss and muscle gain
keywords: body recomposition calculator, recomp calories
calcJS: var w=f('weight');var bf=f('bf')/100;var lbm=w*(1-bf);var factors=[12,14,16,18];var cal=w*factors[Math.min(f('activity')-1,3)];var protein=lbm*1.2;var fat=w*0.4;var carbs=(cal-protein*4-fat*9)/4;return{calories:fmt(cal,0)+' cal',protein:fmt(protein,0)+'g',carbs:fmt(Math.max(carbs,50),0)+'g',fat:fmt(fat,0)+'g'};

faqs.a: Losing fat and building muscle simultaneously. Best suited for beginners, those returning to training, or people with higher body fat. You eat at maintenance or a slight deficit with high protein.
1.0-1.2g per pound of lean body mass is optimal. This is higher than for simple weight loss because you need protein for both fat loss and muscle building.
Yes — the scale may not change much since muscle gain offsets fat loss. Track progress with measurements, photos, and strength gains rather than scale weight.
It is harder for advanced trainees. They may benefit more from traditional bulk/cut cycles. Beginners and intermediates respond best to recomposition.

howItWorksIntro: This body recomposition calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Creatine Dosage Calculator
tagline: Calculate your optimal creatine monohydrate dosage
keywords: creatine calculator, creatine dosage
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var phase=f('phase');var dose=phase===1?w*0.3:w*0.05;var timing=phase===1?'Split into 4 doses throughout the day':'Take with post-workout meal';var water=Math.round(w*0.04);return{daily:fmt(dose,1)+'g',timing:timing,water:water+' liters/day'};

faqs.a: The most researched sports supplement. Creatine monohydrate increases phosphocreatine stores in muscles, improving high-intensity exercise performance by 10-20%. It is safe for long-term use.

faqs.q: Do I need a loading phase?
faqs.a: Loading (0.3g/kg for 5-7 days) saturates muscles faster. Maintenance-only (0.05g/kg daily) works too but takes 3-4 weeks to fully saturate. Both reach the same endpoint.
Timing is not critical. Taking it with food improves absorption slightly. Post-workout with protein and carbs is popular but not required.
Initial weight gain of 2-4 lbs is normal from intracellular water retention. This is not bloating — the water is inside muscle cells, which is beneficial.

howItWorksIntro: This creatine dosage calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Loading: 0.3g per kg bodyweight for 5-7 days
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Bulking Calorie Calculator
tagline: Calculate calories and macros for a lean bulk
keywords: bulking calculator, muscle gain calories
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var h=f('height')*2.54;var age=f('age');var bmr=10*w+6.25*h-5*age+5;var factors=[1.2,1.375,1.55,1.725,1.9];var maintain=bmr*factors[Math.min(f('activity')-1,4)];var surplus=maintain*0.1;var bulk=maintain+surplus;var protein=w*2.205;return{maintain:fmt(maintain,0)+' cal',bulk:fmt(bulk,0)+' cal',protein:fmt(protein,0)+'g',surplus:fmt(surplus,0)+' cal'};

faqs.q: How many extra calories for bulking?
faqs.a: A lean bulk adds 10-15% above maintenance (250-500 cal/day). This supports muscle growth while minimizing fat gain. Aggressive bulking (500+) gains muscle faster but with more fat.

faqs.q: How much protein for muscle gain?
faqs.a: 1.0g per pound of body weight is the standard recommendation for maximizing muscle protein synthesis during a bulk.

faqs.q: How fast should I gain weight?
faqs.a: 0.5-1 lb per week for beginners, 0.25-0.5 for intermediates. Faster gain means more fat. If gaining over 1 lb/week, you are likely adding unnecessary fat.
Until body fat reaches 15-18% (men) or 25-28% (women), then transition to a cut. Typical bulk lasts 3-6 months.

howItWorksIntro: This bulking calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Cutting Calorie Calculator
tagline: Calculate calories and macros for a cutting phase
keywords: cutting calculator, fat loss calories
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var h=f('height')*2.54;var age=f('age');var bmr=10*w+6.25*h-5*age+5;var factors=[1.2,1.375,1.55,1.725,1.9];var maintain=bmr*factors[Math.min(f('activity')-1,4)];var deficit=500;var cut=maintain-deficit;var protein=f('weight')*1.2;return{maintain:fmt(maintain,0)+' cal',cut:fmt(cut,0)+' cal',protein:fmt(protein,0)+'g',deficit:fmt(deficit,0)+' cal'};

faqs.q: How big of a deficit for cutting?
faqs.a: 500 cal/day deficit for 1 lb/week loss. Aggressive cuts (750-1000) lose faster but risk muscle loss. Never go below BMR.
Higher than bulking: 1.1-1.3g per pound bodyweight. Extra protein preserves muscle mass during a caloric deficit.
Until reaching 10-12% body fat (men) or 18-22% (women). Typical cuts last 8-16 weeks. Take diet breaks every 6-8 weeks if needed.
Some muscle loss is inevitable, but high protein, heavy lifting, and moderate deficit minimize it. Crash dieting causes significant muscle loss.

howItWorksIntro: This cutting calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Protein = 1.2g per pound bodyweight during a cut
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Gym Membership Cost Calculator
tagline: Compare gym membership costs and find the best value
keywords: gym membership cost, gym price comparison
calcJS: var m=f('monthly');var init=f('initFee');var visits=f('visits');var months=f('months');var total=m*months+init;var perVisit=total/(visits*months);var perDay=total/(months*30);return{totalCost:$(total),perVisit:$(perVisit),perDay:$(perDay)};

faqs.q: What is the average gym membership cost?
faqs.a: Budget gyms: $10-30/month. Mid-range: $30-60/month. Premium: $60-200/month. CrossFit/boutique: $150-300/month. Most gyms charge a one-time initiation fee.

faqs.q: How do I get the best deal?
faqs.a: Sign up in January (New Year sales), negotiate the initiation fee (it is almost always negotiable), ask for a free trial first, and check if your employer or insurance offers gym discounts.

faqs.q: Is a gym membership worth it?
faqs.a: At $50/month and 15 visits, that is $3.33 per visit. Cheaper than most fitness classes ($15-30), personal training ($60-150/session), or boutique studios.
Watch for annual maintenance fees ($30-50), cancellation fees, personal training upsells, and automatic renewal. Read the full contract carefully.

howItWorksIntro: This gym membership cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Total Cost = Monthly × Months + Initiation Fee
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Blood Pressure Calculator
tagline: Check your blood pressure reading against health guidelines
keywords: blood pressure calculator, bp categories
calcJS: var sys=f('systolic');var dia=f('diastolic');var cat,risk,action;if(sys<120&&dia<80){cat='Normal';risk='Low';action='Maintain healthy lifestyle';}else if(sys<130&&dia<80){cat='Elevated';risk='Moderate';action='Lifestyle changes recommended';}else if(sys<140||dia<90){cat='High Blood Pressure Stage 1';risk='High';action='Lifestyle changes + consider medication';}else if(sys>=140||dia>=90){cat='High Blood Pressure Stage 2';risk='Very High';action='Medication + lifestyle changes';}else{cat='Hypertensive Crisis';risk='Dangerous';action='Seek emergency care immediately';}if(sys>=180||dia>=120){cat='Hypertensive Crisis';risk='Dangerous';action='Call 911 immediately';}return{category:cat,risk:risk,action:action};

faqs.a: Normal is below 120/80 mmHg. The top number (systolic) measures pressure when the heart beats. The bottom (diastolic) measures pressure between beats.
Risk factors include age, family history, obesity, high sodium diet, lack of exercise, stress, smoking, and excessive alcohol. Often called the silent killer because it has no symptoms.
At least once a year for adults with normal BP. More frequently if elevated. Home monitoring with a validated cuff is recommended for those with high BP.
Yes — reducing sodium to under 2,300mg/day, exercising 150 min/week, maintaining healthy weight, limiting alcohol, and managing stress can lower BP by 5-15 mmHg.

howItWorksIntro: This blood pressure calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Categories from American Heart Association 2024 guidelines
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: A1C Calculator
tagline: Convert between A1C and average blood glucose levels
keywords: a1c calculator, blood sugar to a1c, hba1c
calcJS: var a1c=f('a1c');var eag=28.7*a1c-46.7;var mmol=eag/18;var cat=a1c<5.7?'Normal':a1c<6.5?'Prediabetes':'Diabetes';return{avgGlucose:fmt(eag,0)+' mg/dL',category:cat,mgPerL:fmt(mmol,1)+' mmol/L'};

faqs.a: A1C (HbA1c) measures average blood sugar over the past 2-3 months by looking at the percentage of hemoglobin with attached sugar. It is the primary test for diabetes diagnosis and management.
Normal: below 5.7%. Prediabetes: 5.7-6.4%. Diabetes: 6.5% or higher. For diabetics, the ADA target is generally below 7%.

faqs.q: How does A1C relate to blood sugar?
faqs.a: A1C of 6% = average glucose of ~126 mg/dL. Each 1% change in A1C equals about 29 mg/dL change in average glucose.

faqs.q: How often should A1C be tested?
faqs.a: Twice yearly for well-controlled diabetes. Quarterly if treatment changed or not meeting goals. Annually for non-diabetics with risk factors.

howItWorksIntro: This a1c calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Caffeine Half-Life Calculator
tagline: Track how long caffeine stays in your system
keywords: caffeine calculator, caffeine half life
calcJS: var mg=f('amount');var hrs=f('time');var halfLife=5;var remaining=mg*Math.pow(0.5,hrs/halfLife);var clearHrs=Math.log(25/mg)/Math.log(0.5)*halfLife;return{remaining:fmt(remaining,0)+' mg',halfTime:'5 hours (average)',clearTime:fmt(Math.max(0,clearHrs-hrs),1)+' hours from now'};

faqs.a: Caffeine half-life is about 5 hours for most adults. This means if you drink 200mg, you still have 100mg in your system 5 hours later. It varies by genetics, age, and liver function.
Caffeine consumed within 6 hours of bedtime significantly disrupts sleep quality, even if you fall asleep fine. The rule of thumb: stop caffeine 8-10 hours before bed.

faqs.q: How much caffeine is in common drinks?
faqs.a: Coffee (8oz): 95mg. Espresso: 63mg. Black tea: 47mg. Green tea: 28mg. Cola: 34mg. Energy drink: 80-300mg. Caffeine pills: usually 200mg.
Yes — regular consumption reduces its stimulant effects. A 1-2 week break (caffeine fast) resets tolerance. Withdrawal symptoms (headache, fatigue) last 2-9 days.

howItWorksIntro: This caffeine half-life calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Vitamin D Calculator
tagline: Calculate your recommended vitamin D supplementation
keywords: vitamin d calculator, vitamin d dosage
calcJS: var lvl=f('level');var w=f('weight');var target=f('target');var status=lvl<20?'Deficient':lvl<30?'Insufficient':lvl<50?'Adequate':'Optimal';var gap=target-lvl;var dose=gap>20?5000:gap>10?3000:gap>0?2000:1000;var months=Math.ceil(gap/8);return{status:status+' ('+fmt(lvl,0)+' ng/mL)',dailyDose:fmt(dose,0)+' IU',timeToTarget:fmt(Math.max(1,months),0)+' months'};

faqs.q: How much vitamin D do I need?
faqs.a: The RDA is 600-800 IU, but many experts recommend 2,000-5,000 IU daily to reach optimal levels of 40-60 ng/mL. Those with deficiency may need higher doses temporarily.

faqs.q: What is a good vitamin D level?
faqs.a: Under 20 ng/mL is deficient. 20-29 is insufficient. 30-50 is adequate. 40-60 is considered optimal by many functional medicine practitioners. Over 100 is potentially toxic.
About 42% of US adults are vitamin D deficient. Risk factors: dark skin, northern latitude, obesity, limited sun exposure, and being over 65.

faqs.q: Can I get enough from sun?
faqs.a: 15-20 minutes of midday sun on arms and legs produces about 10,000 IU. However, sunscreen, latitude, season, and skin color affect production. Most people need supplementation.

howItWorksIntro: This vitamin d calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Dose based on gap between current and target levels
Average increase: ~8 ng/mL per month at 5000 IU/day
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Cholesterol Ratio Calculator
tagline: Calculate your cholesterol ratios and heart disease risk
keywords: cholesterol ratio calculator, hdl ldl ratio
calcJS: var total=f('total');var hdl=f('hdl');var ldl=f('ldl');var trig=f('trig');var ratio=total/hdl;var ldlHdl=ldl/hdl;var trigHdl=trig/hdl;var risk=ratio<3.5?'Low':ratio<5?'Moderate':'High';return{ratio:fmt(ratio,1),ldlHdl:fmt(ldlHdl,1),trigHdl:fmt(trigHdl,1),risk:risk};

faqs.q: What is a good Total/HDL ratio?
faqs.a: Below 3.5 is ideal. 3.5-5.0 is acceptable. Above 5.0 indicates elevated cardiovascular risk. This ratio is often more predictive than total cholesterol alone.

faqs.q: Why does the Trig/HDL ratio matter?
faqs.a: Trig/HDL under 2.0 suggests healthy insulin sensitivity. Over 3.0 may indicate insulin resistance and metabolic syndrome, even with otherwise normal cholesterol.

faqs.q: How do I improve my ratios?
faqs.a: Raise HDL: exercise, omega-3s, moderate alcohol. Lower LDL: reduce saturated fat, eat fiber, consider statins. Lower triglycerides: reduce sugar, alcohol, and refined carbs.
Every 4-6 years for adults with normal levels. Annually if levels are abnormal or you have risk factors. More often if on cholesterol medication.

howItWorksIntro: This cholesterol ratio calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Dog Age Calculator
tagline: Convert your dog's age to human years using the latest science
keywords: dog age calculator, dog years
calcJS: var da=f('dogAge');var sz=f('size');var factors={1:[15,9,4.5],2:[15,9,5],3:[15,9,6],4:[15,9,7.5]};var ft=factors[sz]||factors[2];var ha=da<=1?da*ft[0]:da<=2?ft[0]+ft[1]:ft[0]+ft[1]+(da-2)*ft[2];var stage=da<0.5?'Puppy':da<2?'Junior':da<7?'Adult':'Senior';var life=sz===1?14:sz===2?12:sz===3?10:8;return{humanAge:fmt(ha,0)+' human years',stage:stage,lifeExpect:life+' years'};

faqs.a: The old 7:1 rule is inaccurate. Dogs mature much faster in year 1 (equivalent to 15 human years) and the rate varies by size. Large dogs age faster than small dogs.

faqs.q: Why do small dogs live longer?
faqs.a: Large dogs age faster at a cellular level and are more prone to cancer and joint disease. Small breeds like Chihuahuas can live 15-20 years, while Great Danes average 7-8.

faqs.q: When is my dog considered senior?
faqs.a: Small dogs: 10-12 years. Medium: 8-10. Large: 6-8. Giant: 5-6. Senior dogs need more frequent vet checkups and adjusted nutrition.
Breed, size, genetics, diet, exercise, veterinary care, and dental health. Mixed breeds tend to live slightly longer than purebreds on average.

howItWorksIntro: This dog age calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: After age 2: +4.5-7.5 years per dog year depending on size
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Cat Age Calculator
tagline: Convert your cat's age to human years
keywords: cat age calculator, cat years
calcJS: var ca=f('catAge');var ha=ca<=1?15:ca<=2?24:24+(ca-2)*4;var stage=ca<0.5?'Kitten':ca<2?'Junior':ca<7?'Prime':ca<11?'Mature':ca<15?'Senior':'Geriatric';return{humanAge:fmt(ha,0)+' human years',stage:stage};

faqs.a: Indoor cats average 12-18 years. Outdoor cats average 5-10 years due to traffic, predators, and disease. Some indoor cats live past 20.

faqs.q: When is a cat considered senior?
faqs.a: Cats are considered senior at 11 years (about 60 human years). They may need more frequent vet visits and dietary adjustments.
Indoor cats tend to age more slowly due to less environmental stress. They need stimulation through play and enrichment to stay healthy.

faqs.q: What is the oldest cat on record?
faqs.a: Creme Puff from Texas lived to 38 years old, equivalent to about 168 human years.

howItWorksIntro: This cat age calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: After 2: +4 human years per cat year
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Dog Calorie Calculator
tagline: Calculate your dog's daily calorie needs
keywords: dog calorie calculator, how much to feed dog
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var rer=70*Math.pow(w,0.75);var factors=[[2,1.6,1.4,1.2],[3,1.6,1.8,2.5],[1.4,1.2,1.4,1.6]];var ageIdx=Math.min(f('age')-1,2);var actIdx=Math.min(f('activity')-1,3);var cal=rer*factors[ageIdx][actIdx];var cups=cal/350;var meals=f('age')===1?3:2;return{calories:fmt(cal,0)+' cal/day',cups:fmt(cups,1)+' cups',meals:meals+' meals/day'};

faqs.a: We use the Resting Energy Requirement (RER) formula: 70 × (body weight in kg)^0.75, then multiply by an activity/life stage factor.

faqs.q: How much should I feed my dog?
faqs.a: Follow calorie guidelines rather than cup measurements, as kibble calorie density varies. Check your food's calories per cup and divide daily needs accordingly.

faqs.q: How do I know if my dog is overweight?
faqs.a: You should feel ribs easily without pressing hard. A visible waist when viewed from above and a tucked abdomen from the side indicate healthy weight.

faqs.q: Should I feed once or twice a day?
faqs.a: Adult dogs do well with 2 meals per day. Puppies need 3-4 meals. Large breeds prone to bloat may benefit from 2-3 smaller meals rather than one large one.

howItWorksIntro: This dog calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Cat Food Calculator
tagline: Calculate your cat's daily food needs
keywords: cat food calculator, how much to feed cat
calcJS: var w=f('weight')*0.453592;var rer=70*Math.pow(w,0.75);var factors=[[1.2,1.4,1.6],[2.5,1.4,1.6],[1,1.2,1.4]];var ageIdx=Math.min(f('age')-1,2);var actIdx=Math.min(f('activity')-1,2);var cal=rer*factors[ageIdx][actIdx];var wet=cal/30;var dry=cal/350;return{calories:fmt(cal,0)+' cal/day',wet:fmt(wet,1)+' oz',dry:fmt(dry,2)+' cups'};

faqs.q: How many calories does a cat need?
faqs.a: Average indoor adult cat: 200-250 calories/day. Active cats may need 300+. Kittens need more calories per pound for growth.
Wet food provides hydration (important for cats who do not drink enough water) and is typically lower in carbs. Dry food is convenient and helps dental health. A mix is ideal.

faqs.q: How often should I feed my cat?
faqs.a: Adult cats: 2 meals per day or measured free-feeding. Kittens: 3-4 meals per day. Avoid unlimited free-feeding as it leads to obesity.
Over 50% of US cats are overweight. A healthy cat has a visible waist, you can feel ribs without pressing hard, and has minimal belly pouch.

howItWorksIntro: This cat food calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Daily Calories = RER × Life Stage Factor
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Puppy Weight Predictor
tagline: Predict your puppy's adult weight
keywords: puppy weight predictor, how big will my puppy get
calcJS: var cw=f('currentWeight');var age=f('currentAge');var sz=f('breed');var matureWeeks=[40,48,60,72,96];var mw=matureWeeks[sz-1]||60;var pctGrown=Math.min(age/mw,1);var adult=pctGrown>0?cw/pctGrown:cw*4;var doneMonths=Math.round(mw/4.33);return{adultWeight:fmt(adult,0)+' lbs',growthPct:pct(pctGrown*100,0),doneGrowing:doneMonths+' months'};

faqs.a: Toy breeds: 8-10 months. Small: 10-12 months. Medium: 12-15 months. Large: 15-18 months. Giant breeds: 18-24 months.
Weight predictions are approximate. Genetics, nutrition, and neutering status all affect final size. Knowing the parents' weights gives the best estimate.
Neutering before maturity can slightly increase adult size because growth plates close later. The effect is typically 5-10% more than intact dogs.
Puppies should gain weight steadily. A general rule: they should double birth weight by 1 week and reach about 50% of adult weight by 4-5 months.

howItWorksIntro: This puppy weight predictor uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Adult Weight ≈ Current Weight / (Current Age / Mature Age)
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Pet Insurance Cost Calculator
tagline: Estimate pet insurance costs and determine if it saves money
keywords: pet insurance cost, pet insurance worth it
calcJS: var sz=f('breed');var age=f('age');var cov=f('coverage');var ded=f('deductible');var base=cov===1?15:cov===2?40:65;var ageFactor=1+age*0.08;var szFactor=sz===1?0.85:sz===2?1:1.3;var monthly=base*ageFactor*szFactor;var annual=monthly*12;var be=(annual+ded)/0.8;return{monthly:$(monthly),annual:$(annual),breakEven:$(be)};

faqs.a: If your pet has a major illness or injury ($3,000-$10,000+), insurance pays for itself. About 1 in 3 pets needs emergency care each year. Insurance prevents financial-based medical decisions.
As early as possible — premiums are lowest for young pets and pre-existing conditions are excluded. Enrolling at adoption is ideal.
Pre-existing conditions (anything diagnosed before enrollment), cosmetic procedures, breeding costs, and preventive care (unless you buy a wellness add-on).
You pay the vet, submit a claim, and get reimbursed 70-90% after deductible. Some plans now offer direct vet payment.

howItWorksIntro: This pet insurance cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Pet Cost of Ownership Calculator
tagline: Calculate the total annual and lifetime cost of pet ownership
keywords: pet cost calculator, cost of owning a dog
calcJS: var monthly=f('food')+f('insurance')+f('grooming')+f('supplies');var annual=monthly*12+f('vetAnnual');var lifetime=annual*f('lifespan');return{monthly:$(monthly),annual:$(annual),lifetime:$(lifetime)};

faqs.q: How much does a dog cost per year?
faqs.a: Average: $1,500-$3,000/year for a medium dog. This includes food ($500-1,200), vet ($500-1,000), insurance ($300-600), grooming ($200-600), and supplies ($200-500).

faqs.q: What are the biggest hidden costs?
faqs.a: Emergency vet visits ($1,000-5,000+), dental cleanings ($300-800), training classes ($200-600), boarding/pet sitting ($30-75/night), and pet deposits on rentals.

faqs.q: How much does a cat cost per year?
faqs.a: Average: $1,000-$2,000/year. Cats are generally cheaper than dogs due to lower food costs, no grooming needs, and fewer emergency vet visits.

faqs.q: How can I reduce pet costs?
faqs.a: Buy food in bulk, keep up with preventive care (prevents costly emergencies), learn basic grooming, and compare vet prices. Pet insurance can save money on unexpected bills.

howItWorksIntro: This pet cost of ownership calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Annual Cost = (Monthly Expenses × 12) + Annual Vet Bills
Lifetime Cost = Annual Cost × Expected Lifespan
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Ovulation Calculator
tagline: Predict your most fertile days based on your cycle length
keywords: ovulation calculator, fertility calculator, when do I ovulate
calcJS: var cycle=f('cycleDay1');var since=f('lastPeriod');var ovDay=cycle-14;var daysToOv=ovDay-since;var fertileStart=ovDay-5;var fertileEnd=ovDay+1;var nextPeriod=cycle-since;return{ovulationDay:'Day '+fmt(ovDay,0)+' of cycle (in '+fmt(Math.max(0,daysToOv),0)+' days)',fertileWindow:'Day '+fmt(fertileStart,0)+' to Day '+fmt(fertileEnd,0),nextPeriod:'In '+fmt(Math.max(0,nextPeriod),0)+' days'};

faqs.a: Ovulation typically occurs 14 days before your next period. For a 28-day cycle, that is day 14. For a 32-day cycle, around day 18. Ovulation day varies with cycle length.
You can conceive during the 5 days before ovulation and the day of ovulation itself (6 days total). Sperm can survive up to 5 days in the reproductive tract.
It provides estimates based on average cycle patterns. For more accuracy, combine with basal body temperature tracking, cervical mucus observation, or ovulation predictor kits.

faqs.q: What if my cycles are irregular?
faqs.a: Irregular cycles make prediction harder. Use ovulation predictor kits (OPKs) which detect the LH surge 24-36 hours before ovulation for more reliable timing.

howItWorksIntro: This ovulation calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Fertile window = 5 days before ovulation through ovulation day
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Diaper Cost Calculator
tagline: Calculate how much you will spend on diapers from birth to potty training
keywords: diaper cost calculator, diaper budget
calcJS: var cpd=f('costPerDiaper');var dpd=f('diapersPerDay');var months=f('monthsToPotty');var monthly=cpd*dpd*30;var yearly=monthly*12;var total=monthly*months;return{monthly:$(monthly),yearly:$(yearly),total:$(total)};

faqs.q: How many diapers does a baby use?
faqs.a: About 2,500-3,000 in the first year. Newborns use 10-12/day, decreasing to 6-8/day by 12 months. Total from birth to potty training: 6,000-8,000 diapers.
Average: $0.20-0.35 per diaper for disposables. Total cost from birth to potty training: $1,500-$2,500. Cloth diapers cost $300-600 upfront and save long-term.
Average age is 27-32 months, but ranges from 18-36 months. Boys typically train later than girls. Follow the child's readiness signs rather than a fixed age.

faqs.q: How can I save on diapers?
faqs.a: Subscribe-and-save services (15-20% off), buy store brands (30-40% cheaper), use coupons/cashback apps, buy in bulk, and consider cloth diapers for significant savings.

howItWorksIntro: This diaper cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Total = Monthly Cost × Months to Potty Training
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Child Height Predictor
tagline: Predict your child's adult height based on parent heights
keywords: child height predictor, how tall will my child be
calcJS: var dad=f('dadHeight');var mom=f('momHeight');var boy=f('childSex')===1;var mid=(dad+mom)/2;var predicted=boy?mid+2.5:mid-2.5;var lo=predicted-2;var hi=predicted+2;var ft=Math.floor(predicted/12);var inc=Math.round(predicted%12);return{predicted:fmt(predicted,1)+' inches',range:fmt(lo,1)+' - '+fmt(hi,1)+' inches',feetInches:ft+"'"+inc+'"'};

faqs.a: The mid-parent height method predicts within 2-3 inches for about 68% of children. Genetics from grandparents, nutrition, and health can cause variation beyond this range.
For boys: (father + mother) / 2 + 2.5 inches. For girls: (father + mother) / 2 - 2.5 inches. This accounts for average sex differences in height.
Nutrition (especially protein and calcium), sleep quality, physical activity, chronic illness, and genetic factors beyond parents (grandparents, ethnic background).

faqs.q: When do children reach adult height?
faqs.a: Girls typically reach adult height by 14-16, boys by 16-18. A growth spurt during puberty adds the final 3-5 inches.

howItWorksIntro: This child height predictor uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Screen Time Cost Calculator
tagline: See how much time and money your screen habits actually cost
keywords: screen time calculator, phone time cost
calcJS: var h=f('hours');var rate=f('hourlyRate');var weekly=h*7;var yearly=h*365;var cost=yearly*rate;return{weekly:fmt(weekly,0)+' hours',yearly:fmt(yearly,0)+' hours ('+fmt(yearly/24,0)+' days)',oppCost:$(cost),lifetime:$(cost*10)};

faqs.q: How much screen time is normal?
faqs.a: US adults average 7+ hours of daily screen time (including work). Non-work screen time averages 3-5 hours. This has increased 50% since 2020.
Excessive screen time is linked to poor sleep, eye strain, neck/back pain, reduced attention span, increased anxiety and depression, and reduced physical activity.
The American Academy of Pediatrics recommends under 2 hours for children. For adults, reducing non-work screen time to 2-3 hours and taking regular breaks is beneficial.

faqs.q: How can I reduce screen time?
faqs.a: Use built-in screen time trackers, set app limits, enable grayscale mode, create phone-free zones (bedroom, dinner table), and find offline hobbies to replace scrolling.

howItWorksIntro: This screen time cost calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Opportunity Cost = Daily Hours × 365 × Hourly Rate
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Sleep Debt Calculator
tagline: Calculate your accumulated sleep debt and recovery plan
keywords: sleep debt calculator, sleep deficit
calcJS: var ideal=f('idealSleep');var actual=f('actualSleep');var days=f('days');var daily=ideal-actual;var total=daily*days;var recovery=Math.ceil(total/1.5);return{dailyDebt:fmt(daily,1)+' hours',totalDebt:fmt(total,0)+' hours',recoveryTime:fmt(recovery,0)+' days (adding 1.5 hrs/night)'};

faqs.a: The cumulative effect of not getting enough sleep. If you need 8 hours but sleep 6, you accumulate 2 hours of sleep debt per day — 14 hours per week.
Short-term debt (a few days) can be repaid by sleeping extra. Chronic sleep debt (weeks/months) takes longer and may have lasting health effects. You cannot fully bank sleep.

faqs.q: How much sleep do I need?
faqs.a: Most adults need 7-9 hours. The exact amount is genetic. If you need an alarm to wake up, feel tired during the day, or fall asleep within 5 minutes, you likely need more.

faqs.q: What are the effects of sleep debt?
faqs.a: Impaired cognitive function, weakened immune system, increased cortisol, weight gain, higher risk of diabetes, heart disease, depression, and reduced life expectancy.

howItWorksIntro: This sleep debt calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Recovery assumes adding 1.5 extra hours per night
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Burnout Risk Calculator
tagline: Assess your risk of professional burnout
keywords: burnout calculator, burnout risk assessment
calcJS: var wh=f('workHours');var vac=f('vacDays');var stress=f('stressLevel');var sleep=f('sleepHours');var exercise=f('exerciseDays');var score=0;score+=wh>50?30:wh>45?20:wh>40?10:0;score+=vac<5?20:vac<10?10:0;score+=stress*5;score+=sleep<6?20:sleep<7?10:0;score-=exercise*5;score=Math.min(100,Math.max(0,score));var level=score>=70?'High — Take immediate action':score>=40?'Moderate — Make changes soon':'Low — Keep up good habits';var top=wh>50?'Excessive work hours':stress>7?'High stress level':sleep<6?'Insufficient sleep':'Lack of vacation time';return{score:fmt(score,0)+'/100',level:level,topFactor:top};

faqs.a: Burnout is chronic workplace stress that has not been successfully managed. Symptoms include exhaustion, cynicism about work, reduced professional efficacy, and physical symptoms.
Dreading work, chronic fatigue, insomnia, irritability, difficulty concentrating, frequent illness, feeling detached, and declining performance.

faqs.q: How do I recover from burnout?
faqs.a: Set boundaries on work hours, take your vacation time, prioritize sleep, exercise regularly, practice stress management, and consider professional help. Recovery takes weeks to months.

faqs.q: Is burnout the same as depression?
faqs.a: They overlap but are distinct. Burnout is tied to work and improves with rest/vacation. Depression is pervasive and does not improve with time off alone. Both can coexist.

howItWorksIntro: This burnout risk calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Score based on: work hours, vacation use, stress level, sleep, exercise
Higher scores indicate greater burnout risk
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

name: Social Media Addiction Calculator
tagline: Quantify your social media usage and its real cost
keywords: social media addiction calculator, phone addiction
calcJS: var daily=f('dailyMin');var pickups=f('pickups');var bed=f('beforeBed');var yearly=daily*365/60;var days=yearly/24;var lifetime=yearly*50/8760;var score=daily>180?'Heavy — significantly impacting life':daily>120?'High — above average usage':daily>60?'Moderate — typical usage':'Light — below average';return{yearlyHours:fmt(yearly,0)+' hours',yearlyDays:fmt(days,0)+' days',addictionScore:score,lifetimeYears:fmt(lifetime,1)+' years'};

faqs.q: How much social media time is average?
faqs.a: US adults average 2 hours 27 minutes per day on social media (2026). Ages 16-24 average nearly 3 hours. This totals about 6 years of a lifetime.

Yes — platforms are designed to be addictive using variable rewards, social validation, and infinite scroll. The brain's dopamine system responds to likes and notifications similarly to slot machines.

faqs.q: What are the mental health effects?
faqs.a: Research links heavy social media use to increased anxiety, depression, body image issues, sleep disruption, FOMO, and reduced attention span. The effects are strongest in teens.
Delete apps (use browser only), turn off notifications, set daily time limits, create phone-free zones, use grayscale mode, and replace scrolling with a specific hobby.

howItWorksIntro: This social media addiction calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorksRules: Lifetime total assumes 50 years of usage
howItWorksOutro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.
